calendar.matters:
MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to the hearing, counsel appearing specially for Plaintiff appears via Zoom. (See notes for full details) The TR becomes the final ruling of the court since there was no notice to contest the court's TR received last Friday.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s unopposed Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, dismissal is vacated and judgment shall be entered as such: Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. shall recover $4,809.26 in principal and $300 in costs, for a total judgment of $5,109.26. This is the judgment authorized by the settlement agreement and related events.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form.

No proposed order nor proposed judgment received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order and proposed judgment which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
